verdicts.note: Direction rests on a single campaign-site phrase ('jail reform and community safety standards'); the cited bills (firearm background checks, anti-police-impersonation) are direction-neutral on enforcement-vs-prevention. Mapping 'jail reform' to pole_b is indirect inference, which the guide rates low, not medium.

Basis misstates the dossier: 'his legislative page highlights bills protecting immigrants' appears nowhere in it — none of his six listed bills concern immigration, so the S2 citation does not support the claim. The only evidence is one generic pamphlet pledge to keep 'fighting for immigrant rights' [S1], which supports direction but not fully-pole_b (+2 implies constrain-ICE/sanctuary positions the dossier never shows). Single clear statement -> +1 medium, citation S1 only.

Endorsement-only evidence (Planned Parenthood) with no social-policy statements is indirect inference. The scoring file itself rated the identical evidence pattern — Washington Conservation Action endorsement with no stated position — as low on climate; the same standard applies here.

Score is sound, but the highlights bullet lumps SB 5974 in with the bills he 'opposes ... as constitutional violations'; the dossier frames only HB 2165, SB 5925, and HB 2320 that way, listing SB 5974 separately under public safety (elected sheriffs). The bullet should not attribute the constitutional-violation framing to SB 5974.
